title: coronilla ebracteata
intro: Coronilla ebracteata, also known as broom clover or Scotch broom, is a perennial leguminous plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. Coronilla ebracteata has been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving menstrual cramps, and improving urinary function. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat conditions like digestive disorders, fever, and respiratory problems.
